DAVID H. ANDERSON 
ATTORNEY AT LAW 
ENVIRONMENTAL LAW 
PLANNING & ZONING LAW 
ADMINISTRATIVE LAW 
Ms. Janis Shank McLean 
Counsel 
Legal Division 
November 24, 1982 
Fair Political Practices Commission 
P. O. Box 807 
Sacramento, California 95804 
LA ARCADA BUILDING 
1114 STATE STREET, SUITE 310 
SANTA BARBARA, CALIFORNIA 93101 
(805) 963-3588 
Subject: 
Committee for a Responsible Council 
Dear Ms. McLean: 
I am submitting this letter as legal counsel for the Committee for 
a Responsible Council. 
The Committee is a registered campaign 
committee (1D #790078) which has been a very successful "grass 
roots" organization of local homeowners who have backed City 
Council candidates in the three most recent local elections. 
Six 
of the seven current City Council members were supported by this 
group, and endorsements by the Committee for a Responsible Council 
carry a great deal of weight in our community. 
During the most recent election campaign, a new group appeared 
under the name Citizens for a Responsive Council. 
This group is 
backed by certain persons who oppose the positions taken by the 
Committee for a Responsible Council. 
The new group placed ads in 
a local newspaper in opposition to a Charter Amendment which would 
have increased the salary for City Council members. 
The Committee 
for a Responsible Council did not take a position on that measure, 
but a number of people in the community were confused by the 
similarity of names and the measure was defeated. 
What role the 
"copy-cat" group had in that defeat cannot be proven, but it had 
some impact in confusing the voters. 
The confusion created by the use of this "copy-cat" name may have 
the impact of reducing the effectiveness of endorsements by the 
Committee for a Responsible Council in future elections -
a result 
which is exactly what the new group seeks. 
My review of the Political Reform Act does not provide guidance on 
this issue. 
The Committee for a Responsible Council would like to 
take legal action to protect its registered name. 
I am therefore 
requesting your assistance in advising the Committee if legal 
remedies are available to prevent future voter confusion and 
deception. 
Can the new group be required to change its name? 
Can we enjoin the use of that name? 
We understand that your office is presently inundated with complaints 
and investigations arising from the most recent election campaigns. 


DAVID H. ANDERS 
ATTORNEY AT LAW 
Ms. Janis Shank McLean 
November 24, 1982 
Page Two 
I am very appreciative of the guidance and assistance you provided 
to the Santa Barbara County Counsel's Office in response to my 
inquiry regarding the County Planning Commission. 
With the backlog 
of other matters requiring your attention, we would appreciate any 
informal guidance on how we might proceed with this issue, so that 
we can take appropriate steps to prevent future voter deception. 
Thank you very much for your assistance in this matter. 
cc: 
Alice Rypins 
President, CRC 
Raymond Myerson 
Treasurer, CRC 
Enclosures

David H. Anderson

sections.question_synthetic: Whether a registered campaign committee can prevent another organization from using a confusingly similar name that may deceive voters and reduce the effectiveness of the first committee's endorsements.
sections.parsing_notes: Format: ocr; LLM: This is an incoming request letter from an attorney, not an FPPC response. The document includes both the original request (November 24, 1982) and what appears to be FPPC acknowledgment correspondence with a referral notation (A-82-195). OCR quality is poor in some sections, affecting confidence slightly. No formal CONCLUSION section exists as this is the initial request, not the FPPC's response.

embedding.summary: Attorney David H. Anderson requests advice on behalf of the Committee for a Responsible Council (registered campaign committee #790078) regarding whether legal remedies exist to prevent another group, 'Citizens for a Responsive Council,' from using a confusingly similar name that may confuse voters and dilute the committee's political effectiveness.
